expectations.text: Case focus is explicitly covered: modern grid hyperlink-style coverage separates contextual-link styling from ordinary element rendering
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly states the case focus and implements it with separate scenarios for linked vs non-linked cells: “This implies Phase 4a should contain separate scenarios for: 1) Cells/objects that DO have contextual links… 2) Cells/objects that do NOT have contextual links…”. It further includes “Contextual-link cell is visually distinguishable from normal cell” and “Non-linked cell does not look like a hyperlink,” plus a mixed comparison scenario, directly separating contextual-link styling from ordinary rendering.

expectations.text: Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: Both execution_notes.md and result.md claim and follow Phase 4a structure: execution_notes.md: “Phase targeting: ensured output content is Phase 4a style (subcategory-only; no canonical buckets).” result.md: “Per the Phase 4a contract, the draft must be subcategory-first (no canonical top-level buckets)” and presents “Modern Grid — Contextual link styling” with nested scenarios/steps and no canonical categories.
